summary: The session focused on how fishing boats manage rough seas during deep-sea fishing expeditions, including their design, equipment, and crew training. It also covered methods for addressing motion sickness among crew members and the protocols in place for severe cases of seasickness.
facts: Fishing boats designed for deep-sea fishing are larger, heavier, and made from durable materials to withstand harsh sea conditions. Fishing boats utilize a ballast system to adjust their weight for stability in rough seas. Specialized fishing gear, including stronger lines and nets, is used to handle turbulent waters. Fishing boats are equipped with radar and GPS technology for navigation and fish location in rough seas. Reliable communication systems are essential for crew coordination during rough conditions. Experienced crews are crucial for managing boats in challenging sea environments. Crew members can take motion sickness medications like Dramamine or Scopolamine to alleviate symptoms. Natural remedies such as ginger can be used by crew members to combat motion sickness. Staying on deck for fresh air can help alleviate motion sickness symptoms for some crew members. Hydration is important for preventing motion sickness, and crew members should avoid alcohol and caffeine. Eating light, plain, and easily digestible food can help crew members avoid motion sickness. Resting and sleeping before and during the trip can alleviate motion sickness for crew members. Fishing boats typically do not have medical professionals on board but carry first aid kits and emergency medical supplies. If a crew member becomes severely seasick, the captain may provide medical attention or return to shore for hospital care.
